channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: The hidden system behind every Renaissance trial - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on systems underlying Renaissance trials. The episode explores hidden organizational structures and patterns in historical legal proceedings.

item.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
item.description: AWS Developers: AWS Developers presents a 7-step framework for system design interviews that prioritizes problem understanding before technology selection. The framework covers requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints, demonstrated through a URL shortener example.

item.title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Live summit featuring AI leaders discussing federal regulation of frontier models, the Anthropic Fable and Mythos recall, AI infrastructure buildout, enterprise budget priorities, and competitive dynamics between OpenAI and Anthropic. Speakers debate whether regulation accelerates China's advantage and whether compute or outcomes will dominate the next phase of AI development.

item.title: What does the next training paradigm look like?
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel explores the next generation of training paradigms beyond current scaling approaches. The discussion covers the importance of grindability and verifiability, reinforcement learning from verification, model dreaming, and predictions for AI capabilities by 2027.

item.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
item.description: Better Stack: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handle streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLM, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal AI workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to enable natural conversation with interruption support, addressing limitations of sequential STT-LLM-TTS pipelines.

item.title: 5 things every AI agent harness needs
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Muhammad Farooq outlines 5 critical components required for AI agent harnesses to move from demos to shipped products. The emphasis is on the infrastructure and systems that operationalize AI model predictions into finished tasks.

item.title: Gemini Canvas Tutorial: How to Use Google's Most Underrated AI Tool
item.description: AI Master: Gemini Canvas is a feature within Google Gemini that generates working websites, no-code apps, dashboards, and audio podcasts from text prompts. The tutorial covers six levels of Canvas functionality from document editing to advanced multi-feature combinations.

item.title: Learn how Professor David J. Malan realized computer science is the field for him.
item.description: CS50: A video featuring Professor David J. Malan describing how he discovered computer science as his field of study. The content is part of CS50, Harvard University's foundational computer science course, which is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

item.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
item.description: AI Engineer: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that transforms 10,000+ notes, documents, and repositories from an Obsidian vault into live context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture with raw content, indexed knowledge, and a wiki layer to enable efficient token-based memory without relying on vector or graph databases.

item.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
item.description: AICodeKing: Ornith is a new family of open agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, ranging from 9B to 397B parameters. Built on Gemma and Qwen, the models are designed for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows with support for reasoning tags and tool-call blocks. The 35B and 9B variants are optimized for local machine deployment in GGUF format.

item.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: A video demonstration of Venngage AI's one-pager generator, which creates client-ready marketing documents from a single prompt. The workflow covers content generation, design, image selection, editing, and PDF export in approximately 15 minutes.

item.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
item.description: AI Engineer: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov presents a technical talk on building and scaling AI agents in production for government workflows. The session covers agent architecture patterns, evaluation methods, monitoring approaches, tool integration, and engineering practices learned from deploying agents across thousands of state and local governments.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents suffer from severe limitations: they can only view small portions of codebases and lose context between interactions, making them ineffective despite individual competence. He frames this as analogous to a master engineer with amnesia and proposes that solutions like Nx and Polygraph can address these constraints.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: A YouTube video discusses leaked evidence that Anthropic's Fable 5 model may be returning after being pulled due to national security concerns. The analysis cites Claude code leaks, Amazon Bedrock listings, congressional pressure, and development of a successor model called Mythos as signals of regulatory change and possible relaunch.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent infrastructure should be built around durable, append-only session logs rather than orchestration layers and tools. He contends that as models commoditize, the log becomes the differentiating primitive that persists across models and runtimes, enabling properties like durability, continuity, and portability.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) are an inference-time compute paradigm that can be applied to coding agents for higher performance and reliability. The presentation discusses mapping RLM principles to coding agents and demonstrates how Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse guide recursive task-solving with declarable outcomes.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviewed historian and author Ada Palmer on the Dwarkesh Patel podcast. The episode title suggests a historical or philosophical discussion about forgiveness as a historical phenomenon.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video debunking viral claims that GTA 6 uses GPT-6, instead arguing Rockstar built custom AI systems for real-time behavioral simulation, persistent NPC memory, and witness systems via RAGE 9 engine. The creator compares LLM responses and attempts to separate confirmed technical details from leaked rumors.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presents updates to data analytics agents with enhanced capabilities for data engineering, science, and conversational analytics. New developer tools include the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, accessible through Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses production evaluation approaches for autonomous AI agents that go beyond offline benchmarks. The talk covers continuous evaluation pipelines, online vs offline evaluation architectures, human-in-the-loop systems, and methods for detecting drift and hallucinations in agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering work from writing individual lines of code to designing agentic systems that require traditional engineering principles like systems thinking, decomposition, and separation of concerns. She contends that engineers will find fulfillment and retain their value by applying existing engineering discipline to higher-level system-building rather than losing it to automation.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs and Instagram co-founder, discusses Anthropic's frontier models Fable and Mythos being pulled from market following government action. Krieger describes how AI-native product development changes engineering workflows and defends Anthropic's safety positioning as substantive rather than marketing. The interview covers Anthropic's dual role as both platform and product competitor.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud experts argue that AI-native databases are essential for powering intelligent agents with high-fidelity, interconnected context. The session covers AI-powered search, natural language interfaces, multimodal queries, graph RAG, and open standards like Model Context Protocol and agentic IDEs as key enablers for agent deployment.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented hybrid search capabilities in Spanner that combine full-text and vector search for AI-powered applications. The session covered multi-model queries for contextual search and retrieval-augmented generation, with integration into Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented integration methods for Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) that standardize agent connections to data sources like BigQuery, AlloyDB, and Google Maps. The session covered AgentOps for observability into agent performance and cost metrics.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented the Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Agent Registry for orchestrating tools and agents. The system enables secure connection of agents to other agents, tools, and organizational data through Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, including both existing and custom-built servers.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind announces Gemini 3 with upgraded model reasoning, expanded context window processing, and enhanced multimodal tool use capabilities. The presentation focuses on enabling developers to build and scale agentic applications using these new features in Vertex AI.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presents a low-code approach for building AI applications on corporate data using Cloud SQL and AlloyDB. The tutorial demonstrates auto-generated code leveraging Gemini for natural language interfaces, hybrid search, image search, and recommendations without requiring AI expertise.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements in Looker that integrate Gemini AI into business intelligence. The updates include dashboard agents for workflow automation, conversational analytics, and a governed semantic layer to ground AI in trusted data for enterprise use.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements in three NoSQL database services: Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable. The session covered product releases, architectural innovations, customer stories, and roadmaps for supporting AI and agentic applications with high-performance, scalable data solutions.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind presents a Deep research API that powers agents built on Gemini to improve performance on comprehensive research tasks. The session demonstrates how to implement these agent techniques using Google Cloud infrastructure.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new Cloud Run capabilities designed to simplify deployment of AI agents and inference workloads. The updates focus on serverless features that enable seamless scaling and reduce operational overhead for AI-driven applications.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud SQL introduces AI-driven automated health management and fleet-wide insights across MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server. The update emphasizes performance optimization, high availability, security, and cloud migration facilitation through Database Migration Service.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced updates to its agent platform including a unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with Agent Builder featuring no-code/low-code design, new Agent Registry for component distribution, and enterprise governance features. The platform includes an expanded Agent Development Kit, agent-to-agent communication, secure code execution sandbox, advanced observability, evaluation suite, and managed simulation capabilities.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas argues that persona-eval benchmarks fail to detect "Miranda distortion," where AI systems speaking as historical figures reproduce cultural composites from training data rather than primary documents. He proposes an epistemic simulation framework evaluated by domain experts using anachronism detection, documentary consistency, and contextual plausibility rather than fluency metrics.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine has added innovations for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud discusses Kubernetes evolution for AI-scale workloads, emphasizing hardware-aware platforms, infrastructure elasticity, agentic workflow security, and resource optimization. Google frames itself as the leading Kubernetes contributor steering industry direction for 11 consecutive years.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming offered through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare. The course is led by David J. Malan and maintains an active community across multiple social platforms including Discord, GitHub, Reddit, and Slack.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-source coding-focused agentic model with a Mixture-of-Experts architecture and one trillion parameters. The model supports a 256K context window and is accompanied by Kimi Code CLI for repository exploration, file editing, command execution, and testing from the terminal.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a conference held at Moscone West in San Francisco on July 1, 2026. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day, livestreamed online.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held Day 1 at Moscone West in San Francisco on June 30, 2026, featuring keynote sessions and main-stage programming focused on software factories and AI engineering. The event streamed live with sessions running from 9 AM to 5:30 PM PT across multiple blocks.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI advocates building an Agentic OS to complement Claude Code development, arguing that mastering agentic fundamentals like skills, loop engineering, and memory management improves Claude Code outputs. The setup offers customization, visual interfaces, and shareability with non-technical teams.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space discusses a shift toward Meta's harness approaches for evaluating AI systems, suggesting Meta-based testing harnesses are becoming the dominant standard in the industry. The piece presents this as a significant trend shift in AI evaluation methodology.

item.title: Bible as RAG Database
item.description: jacksonastone: A discussion or exploration of using the Bible as a retrieval-augmented generation database. This applies RAG techniques to religious text retrieval and reference systems.

item.title: What I'm Finding About LLM Code Style and Token Costs
item.description: jimmont: The author reports observations about how different code styles affect token consumption when using LLMs. This is a practical analysis of code formatting patterns and their impact on inference cost, based on experimental findings.

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Europe is resisting U.S. export restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment as part of Washington's broader strategy to contain Chinese chip capabilities. ASML CEO notes that current Chinese access is limited to older deep ultraviolet lithography tools from a decade ago, which the MATCH Act would further restrict.

item.title: We'll fight the platform war against Big AI
item.description: aendruk: An author signals intent to challenge the dominance of large AI platforms and incumbent companies. The framing suggests a platform competition dynamic between centralized AI incumbents and alternative approaches.
